steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “To address this problem” directly links the underlined sentence to the problem just identified in the previous sentence. This signals that the sentence's function is to describe a solution—specifically, the method used to overcome the monitoring difficulty.

steps.title: Match the method to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A (‘To describe an approach a team of scientists used to study pollinators’) correctly identifies the underlined sentence's function: it describes the approach scientists used (time-lapse cameras) to study the pollinators.

The other three misidentify the sentence's role:
  • B treats it as questioning a claim
  • C treats it as explaining moth preferences
  • D treats it as announcing findings, which actually appear in the following sentence


That confirms A is correct.
